Southern California Wilderness

Discover
SoCal's Best Trails

From desert dunes to mountain peaks. Your guide to the wild places of Southern California.

Join a Run
22+
Trails
4
Skill Levels
SoCal
Wilderness
Trail Explorer

Choose Your Adventure

From fire roads to rock crawls. Each trail includes rig requirements, navigation links, and real-time status.

Open
Easy to Moderate

Cleghorn Fire Road (2N47)

Cajon Pass / San Bernardino
Distance
12 miles
Time
3-4 hours
Rig
Stock 4WD

Ridge run with incredible views. The main road is 2WD friendly; offshoots require 4x4. Perfect introduction to mountain wheeling with panoramic vistas of the Cajon Pass.

Rig Requirements
Stock 4WD / High-Clearance Recommended
Beginner FriendlyFire RoadScenic ViewsMountain Ridge
Google MapsOpen in onX
Seasonal
Moderate to Advanced

Santiago Peak (Main Divide)

Cleveland National Forest
Distance
18 miles
Time
5-6 hours
Rig
Lifted

The highest point in Orange County. Rutted climbs and steep drop-offs. Epic panoramic views from the summit make the challenge worthwhile. Check weather before attempting.

Rig Requirements
Lifted / 33" Tires / Skid Plates
360 ViewsHigh AltitudeRockyMountain Peak
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Easy

Lytle Creek / Stockton Flats

Lytle Creek, CA
Distance
10 miles
Time
2-3 hours
Rig
Stock 4WD

Perfect for a quick run. Light water crossings and shaded campsites along the creek. Great for families and beginner groups looking to practice water crossing techniques.

Rig Requirements
Stock 4WD / All-Terrain Tires
Creek CrossingsCampingCanyonFamily Friendly
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Advanced

Holcomb Creek

Big Bear / Holcomb Valley
Distance
8 miles
Time
3-5 hours
Rig
Lifted

Iconic rock crawling trail. The Gatekeeper sets the tone - if you can't clear it, turn back. Holcomb Creek crossing and deep ruts make this a must-do for experienced wheelers.

Rig Requirements
Lifted / 35" Tires / Lockers / Skid Plates
Rock CrawlingTechnicalWater CrossingsBig Bear
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Moderate to Advanced

Gold Mountain (3N69)

Big Bear / Baldwin Lake
Distance
12 miles
Time
4-5 hours
Rig
Lifted

Starts easy, ends challenging. The rock garden at the summit tests both driver and rig. Nylon strap recommended - this trail is notorious for body damage. Unmatched summit views of Baldwin Lake.

Rig Requirements
Lifted / 33" Tires / Skid Plates Recommended
Rock GardenHigh Clearance RequiredSummit ViewsBig Bear
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Extreme

Dishpan Springs

Big Bear Lake
Distance
5 miles
Time
4-6 hours
Rig
Heavy Mods

The big leagues. Deep bowls, off-camber climbs, and shelf roads that'll test the best-built rigs. Named for the bathtub-size springs. Bring recovery gear and expect body damage.

Rig Requirements
Heavy Mods / 37"+ Tires / Lockers / Armor / Winch
Extreme Rock CrawlingBody Damage LikelyWinch RequiredBig Bear
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Easy to Moderate

Mojave Road (West/Central)

Mojave National Preserve
Distance
140 miles
Time
2-3 days
Rig
Stock 4WD

Historic desert crossing from Colorado River to Barstow. Sand, lava tubes, volcanic formations, and the iconic Mojave mailbox. Bring extra fuel and water. Multiple access points for shorter sections.

Rig Requirements
Stock 4WD / Extra Fuel / Self-Recovery
Desert CrossingMulti-DayHistoric RouteCamping
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Moderate

Ocotillo Wells SVRA

Ocotillo Wells, CA
Distance
Varies
Time
Full Day
Rig
Stock 4WD

California's largest OHV area. Sand dunes, badlands, mud caves, and slot canyons. Blowsand Hill tests your climbing skills. Great camping at Quinn's Bar or Primitive areas.

Rig Requirements
Stock 4WD / All-Terrain Tires / Flag Required
Sand DunesBadlandsCampingDesert Playground
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Moderate to Advanced

Berdoo Canyon

Joshua Tree National Park
Distance
20 miles
Time
4-6 hours
Rig
High Clearance

Old road through Joshua Tree connecting Berdoo Canyon to Geology Tour Road. Rocky sections in Golden Bee Canyon require careful line choice. Joins Pinyon Well/Malapai Hill trails. Permit required.

Rig Requirements
High Clearance / Skid Plates / Extra Fuel
DesertRock SectionsNational ParkHistoric
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Easy

Coxey Road (10S01)

Big Bear / Fawnskin
Distance
15 miles
Time
2-3 hours
Rig
Stock 4WD

Easy fire road perfect for beginners. Winding through pine forests with multiple spur roads for dispersed camping. Great for testing new rigs or beginners wanting to gain confidence.

Rig Requirements
Stock 4WD / All-Terrain Tires
Forest RoadBeginner FriendlyCampingBig Bear
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Moderate

Rainbow Trail

Big Bear / Baldwin Lake
Distance
8 miles
Time
3-4 hours
Rig
Stock 4WD

Rolling forest trail with steady elevation gains. Winding through pine and cedar groves, this trail offers a gentle challenge with rewarding views of Baldwin Lake and the San Gorgonio peaks.

Rig Requirements
Stock 4WD / 33" Tires Recommended
ForestHill ClimbsScenicBig Bear
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Moderate

Gold Fever Trail

El Dorado County
Distance
12 miles
Time
3-4 hours
Rig
Stock 4WD

Historic gold rush wagon route from 1849. Connects Placerville to Lake Tahoe region. Multiple water crossings and sections that challenged 49ers' wagons. Historic markers and scenic Sierra views.

Rig Requirements
Stock 4WD / High Clearance
Gold Rush HistoryWater CrossingsSierra Nevada
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Easy to Extreme

Dumont Dunes

Death Valley Area
Distance
Varies
Time
Full Day
Rig
4WD

The tallest dunes in California. Competition Hill separates the brave from the willing. Wide-open sand playground with extreme terrain on the steep faces. Camping at roadside sites or competition areas.

Rig Requirements
4WD / Wide Tires / Flag Required / Air Down
Sand DunesCompetition HillCampingOHV Area
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Moderate to Advanced

Thimble Trail

San Jacinto Mountains
Distance
6 miles
Time
2-3 hours
Rig
Lifted

Narrow shelf road winding through pine forest. Steep drop-offs and tight turns require precision. Named for Thimble Peak visible along the route. Challenging without being extreme.

Rig Requirements
Lifted / 32" Tires / Skid Plates
Narrow TrailSteep ClimbsOff-CamberSan Jacinto
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Extreme

John Bull Trail

Big Bear / Holcomb Valley
Distance
6 miles
Time
4-6 hours
Rig
Heavily Modified

The Gatekeeper stops most stock rigs. Deep ruts, massive ledges, and tight squeezes. Legendary for eating body panels. Only attempt with heavily modified rigs and experienced spotters.

Rig Requirements
Heavily Modified / 35"+ Tires / Lockers / Winch / Armor
Rock CrawlingGatekeeperBody Damage LikelyBig Bear
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Moderate to Advanced

Odessa-Doran Loop

Big Bear / Holcomb Valley
Distance
10 miles
Time
3-4 hours
Rig
Lifted

Popular loop connecting historic Holcomb Valley sites. Odessa Campground to Doran Regional Park. Mix of fire road and technical rock sections. Multiple creek crossings and historic ruins.

Rig Requirements
Lifted / 33" Tires / Skid Plates
Rock SectionsCreek CrossingsLoop TrailBig Bear
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Extreme

Outer Limits

Johnson Valley
Distance
4 miles
Time
3-5 hours
Rig
Rock Buggy

Part of the King of the Hammers course. Extreme rock crawling and high-speed desert sections. Home base for Ultra4 racing. Watch lunatics attempt Chocolate Cliffs. Spectating only for most.

Rig Requirements
Rock Buggy / Trophy Truck / Experience Essential
King of the HammersCompetition CourseUltra4Johnson Valley
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Moderate to Advanced

Jack North Trail

Johnson Valley
Distance
12 miles
Time
3-4 hours
Rig
Stock 4WD

Rolling desert trail with scenic views of Johnson Valley and surrounding ranges. Mix of sandy whoops and rocky sections. Great warm-up for nearby extreme trails. Good sunset views.

Rig Requirements
Stock 4WD / 33" Tires / Skid Plates
DesertRock SectionsJohnson ValleyScenic
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Advanced

Big Johnson

Johnson Valley
Distance
8 miles
Time
2-4 hours
Rig
Lifted

Named with humor, this trail delivers serious challenges. Steep rock ledges and tight squeezes test articulation and approach angles. Popular for Saturday afternoon wheeling.

Rig Requirements
Lifted / 35" Tires / Lockers
Rock CrawlingLedgesJohnson ValleyTechnical
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Easy

Idyllwild Mountain Road

San Jacinto Mountains
Distance
18 miles
Time
2-3 hours
Rig
Any Vehicle

Scenic mountain road from Hemet to Idyllwild. Paved then graded dirt through pine forests of the San Jacinto Mountains. Cool escape from summer heat. Multiple overlook viewpoints.

Rig Requirements
Any Vehicle / Pavement Ends
Scenic DriveForestSan JacintoBeginner
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Moderate to Advanced

Truckhaven Trail

Salton Sea Area
Distance
14 miles
Time
4-5 hours
Rig
4WD

Historic route through badlands near the Salton Sea. Winding climbs and descents with dramatic valley views. Named for the truck that first conquered the terrain in the 1920s.

Rig Requirements
4WD / All-Terrain Tires / Extra Fuel
DesertHill ClimbsSalton Sea ViewsOff-Camber
Google MapsOpen in onX
Open
Easy to Moderate

Azusa Canyon (East Fork)

San Gabriel Mountains
Distance
10 miles
Time
2-3 hours
Rig
Stock 4WD

Mountain canyon following the East Fork of the San Gabriel River. Multiple water crossings and shaded campsites. Popular weekend spot. Bridge Out means actual river crossings required.

Rig Requirements
Stock 4WD / High Clearance
River CrossingsCampingSan GabrielFamily Friendly
Google MapsOpen in onX
Community

Upcoming Trail Runs

Join the community. Meet fellow wheelers, explore new trails, and make memories. All skill levels welcome.

Mar
28
Sat
Beginner

🌅 Sunset Ridge Run

4:00 PM
Meet at: Cleghorn Trailhead

Join us for a scenic evening run up Cleghorn Ridge. We'll catch sunset at the overlook and have a group campfire at the top. Bring your own firewood and beverages.

Organized by:@noah2131
7 / 15 rigs joined
Apr
5
Sun
Beginner

🇺🇸 Vet & First Responder Meetup

9:00 AM
Meet at: Lytle Creek Ranger Station

Monthly meetup for veterans, active duty, and first responders. Family friendly. We'll run Lytle Creek and have a BBQ lunch at the campground after. All skill levels welcome.

Organized by:@socal_crwlr
12 / 20 rigs joined
Apr
12
Sun
Advanced

⛰️ Santiago Peak Challenge

8:00 AM
Meet at: Main Divide Truck Trail

Advanced run to the highest point in Orange County. Lifted rigs with skids required. We'll tackle the rocky sections as a group. Recovery gear mandatory. Experienced spotters on hand.

Organized by:@rugged_repair
5 / 8 rigs joined
Support

Recovery Resources

Stuck, broken, or need a tow? These trail angels have your back. All volunteers — be respectful and compensate for their time/fuel.

SoCal Crwlr

Full-service recovery throughout Southern California OHV areas. Winch, tow straps, hi-lift, and recovery rig ready to roll.

WinchTow StrapsHi-LiftRecovery RigAll SoCal
@socal_crwlr

Rugged Repair

Mobile Tech

Trail-side repairs and mobile mechanic services. Welding, tools, and expertise to get you rolling again. No trail too remote.

Mobile RepairWeldingToolsTrail-SideRecovery
@rugged_repair
Essential Resources

California Off-Road Resources

Official sources for trail conditions, permits, and responsible recreation. Always verify closures before heading out.

BLM California

Bureau of Land Management OHV areas, permits, and trail maps for California.

Visit Site

State Parks OHMVR

California State Parks Off-Highway Motor Vehicle Recreation division.

Visit Site

Tread Lightly!

National nonprofit promoting responsible outdoor recreation through ethics education.

Visit Site

USFS Alerts

San Bernardino National Forest alerts, closures, and fire restrictions.

Visit Site

DISCLAIMER: Off-roading is dangerous. Trail data is for informational purposes only. Users assume all risk of injury, death, or property damage. Always verify trail closures and conditions with USFS, BLM, or appropriate land management agencies before travel. Practice Tread Lightly principles: travel only where permitted, respect the rights of others, avoid sensitive areas, and do your part to keep trails open.

🌲

SoCal Off-Roaders

Tread lightly. Leave no trace.
